stem: A factory produces pharmaceutical tablets using two sources. Batch Process Alpha handles 56% of production with a known defect rate of 1.5%. Batch Process Beta handles 44% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 7.8% and 13.1%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected pharmaceutical tablet is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Batch Process Beta?

ground_truth.answer: P(Batch Process Beta | defective) ranges from 0.803 to 0.873 as Batch Process Beta's defect rate varies from 7.8% to 13.1%. Point estimate at 10.5%: 0.846.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.803, 0.873] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
